item.title: BAFTA Fellowship acceptance speech at the BAFTA Television Awards in May 2024
item.dc:creator: Rob

item.title: Expanding Horizons: My Continued Journey with Champions Speakers
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Working with Champions Speakers over the past five years has been nothing short of extraordinary. As someone who has spent a lifetime championing diversity, inclusion, and the power of resilience, I’ve always sought platforms that understand the importance of purpose-driven storytelling. Champions is one such platform — a leading UK keynote speakers agency that not only amplifies […]
item.content:encoded:
Working with Champions Speakers over the past five years has been nothing short of extraordinary. As someone who has spent a lifetime championing diversity, inclusion, and the power of resilience, I’ve always sought platforms that understand the importance of purpose-driven storytelling. Champions is one such platform — a leading UK keynote speakers agency that not only amplifies voices but aligns with my mission to make lasting change.

Together, we’ve delivered powerful messages to global leaders and trailblazing organisations including BlackRock, Norton Rose Fulbright (NRF), Superior Care, Wolmer and Partners LLP, and Infineum. These engagements have been about more than just speaking — they’ve been about sparking meaningful conversations and driving transformation from boardrooms to communities.

That’s why I’m thrilled to share that our partnership is entering an exciting new chapter. This extended collaboration with Champions Speakers, and their auxiliarry arms, The motivational Speakers Agency and the Diversity and Inclusion speakers agency will allow us to reach even more audiences, both across the UK and internationally. We’re expanding our reach, entering new markets, and developing even more dynamic opportunities to bring the values of inclusion, leadership, and empowerment to the forefront.

In my view, true leadership is about leaving the world a better place than you found it. And with Champions, I’ve found a team that not only believes in that vision but actively works to bring it to life.

“Together, we are not just booking talks — we are building bridges, breaking barriers, and inspiring a generation to believe in the power of their own voice.” Baroness Floella Benjamin

I’m so grateful to be continuing this journey with such a passionate and professional team.

Jack Hayes, Director at Champions Speakers, shares my excitement:

“Working with Floella has been a privilege. She brings unparalleled passion, wisdom, and authenticity to every event. This new phase of our partnership is about scale and impact — reaching more organisations and continuing to change lives through powerful storytelling.”

There is still so much work to be done — but with strong, values-driven partnerships like this one, the future is bright.

Let’s keep moving forward.

— Floella

item.title: BBC Radio 4 Today programme
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: It was a joy to guest edit the BBC Radio 4 Today programme over the Christmas period, it gave me the opportunity to expand on my mantra which is ‘Childhood lasts a lifetime’. I explored one of the biggest influences on children’s lives… media content. It was important to me to highlight the crisis which […]
item.content:encoded: It was a joy to guest edit the BBC Radio 4 Today programme over the Christmas period, it gave me the opportunity to expand on my mantra which is ‘Childhood lasts a lifetime’. I explored one of the biggest influences on children’s lives… media content. It was important to me to highlight the crisis which is happening because of the lack of quality of older children’s content. Many are migrating to online platforms and watching inappropriate adult material. My guests, including the Secretary of State Lisa Nandy MP, author Malorie Blackman and the Head of Children’s BBC Patricia Hidalgo, also expressed their concerns. The positive response from the public and the media has been enormous.

item.title: Front cover of Mayfair Times
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I am excited to be on the front cover of Mayfair Times and also to be featured in an article for this glamorous magazine. It can be found online at https://edition.pagesuite-professional.co.uk/html5/reader/production/default.aspx?pubname=&edid=d348c04d-19c5-4f95-ba8a-70f39c6f66b2
item.content:encoded: I am excited to be on the front cover of Mayfair Times and also to be featured in an article for this glamorous magazine. It can be found online at https://edition.pagesuite-professional.co.uk/html5/reader/production/default.aspx?pubname=&edid=d348c04d-19c5-4f95-ba8a-70f39c6f66b2

item.title: Coming to England – The Musical
item.dc:creator: Rob

item.title: BAFTA Fellowship
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I am absolutely thrilled and delighted to have received the BAFTA Fellowship Award and feel totally blessed. Just wish my mum and dad were alive to celebrate this part of our family’s Windrush journey.
item.content:encoded: I am absolutely thrilled and delighted to have received the BAFTA Fellowship Award and feel totally blessed. Just wish my mum and dad were alive to celebrate this part of our family’s Windrush journey.

item.title: BIRMINGHAM REP HUB NAMED AFTER ME
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I am proud to have the Young Rep Hub named after me and to be the Patron of Youth and Education at Birmingham Rep, which is a wonderful privilege. I hope to be an inspiration to everyone in the youth theatre as I want children and young people from all cultures and backgrounds to have […]
item.content:encoded: I am proud to have the Young Rep Hub named after me and to be the Patron of Youth and Education at Birmingham Rep, which is a wonderful privilege. I hope to be an inspiration to everyone in the youth theatre as I want children and young people from all cultures and backgrounds to have the opportunity to explore creativity and use their imaginations through Arts and Culture. To me this is social mobility at its best and hopefully attending The Young Rep Hub will encourage and nurture the next generation, not only to be involved in the creative industries, but to give them the confidence to approach anything they do in life.

item.title: BAFTA Fellowship
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: My heart is overflowing with joy and happiness at this news. I am thrilled to be receiving so much love and affection from everyone, especially my Playschool Babies about this wonderful accolade! Follow this link for my interview with Jeriah Kibusi about the announcement.
item.content:encoded:
My heart is overflowing with joy and happiness at this news. I am thrilled to be receiving so much love and affection from everyone, especially my Playschool Babies about this wonderful accolade!

Follow this link for my interview with Jeriah Kibusi about the announcement.

item.title: HARPER’S BAZAAR WOMEN OF THE YEAR
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I was super thrilled to be named as a Woman of the Year by Harper’s Bazaar in 2020
item.content:encoded: I was super thrilled to be named as a Woman of the Year by Harper’s Bazaar in 2020

item.title: EUROPEAN DIVERSITY AWARDS
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I was honoured and overjoyed to receive the European Diversity Lifetime Award in 2023 for my contribution to ‘Diversity and Inclusion’ for the last 50 years.
item.content:encoded: I was honoured and overjoyed to receive the European Diversity Lifetime Award in 2023 for my contribution to ‘Diversity and Inclusion’ for the last 50 years.

item.title: MULTI-CULTURAL BUSINESS & COMMUNITY AWARDS
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I was declared as the ‘Motivational Queen’ in 2022 and awarded the Multi-Cultural Business & Community Lifetime Achievement Award in Birmingham
item.content:encoded: I was declared as the ‘Motivational Queen’ in 2022 and awarded the Multi-Cultural Business & Community Lifetime Achievement Award in Birmingham

item.title: WOMEN IN FILM & TELEVISION LIFETIME AWARD
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I was honoured to receive the Women in Film & Television Lifetime Award for my contribution to the creative industry. I was presented tome my one of my Playschool Babies, Nikki Amuka-Bird. I played her mum in the BBC drama ‘Line of Beauty’ and she told me I inspired her when he was a little […]
item.content:encoded: I was honoured to receive the Women in Film & Television Lifetime Award for my contribution to the creative industry. I was presented tome my one of my Playschool Babies, Nikki Amuka-Bird. I played her mum in the BBC drama ‘Line of Beauty’ and she told me I inspired her when he was a little girl so it was very appropriate.

item.title: TOP 50 WOMEN INFLUENCE LIST
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I was thrilled to be included in the Independent Women 2024 – Top 50 Influence List, for my contribution to many different issues in politics in the House of Lords. These included speaking out about the delays in the Windrush Compensation Scheme and demanding legislation to prevent children from easily accessing online pornography. This is […]
item.content:encoded: I was thrilled to be included in the Independent Women 2024 – Top 50 Influence List, for my contribution to many different issues in politics in the House of Lords. These included speaking out about the delays in the Windrush Compensation Scheme and demanding legislation to prevent children from easily accessing online pornography. This is something I have spent 12 years campaigning for. Finally the government has agreed to bring in legislation to protect our children from online harms. But the battle still goes on.

item.title: My autobiography: What are you doing here?
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: There is also a paperback and audio book version. It was very traumatic reading the book out loud especially the part when my mum left my sister and I and my two brothers, behind back in Trinidad with some horrible foster parents. We were part of the left behind children back in the Caribbean who […]
item.content:encoded: There is also a paperback and audio book version. It was very traumatic reading the book out loud especially the part when my mum left my sister and I and my two brothers, behind back in Trinidad with some horrible foster parents. We were part of the left behind children back in the Caribbean who suffered greatly with no love and affection shown to us. The book is also full of joyful moments and anecdotes of my amazing experiences, which includes meeting so many wonderful, inspirational people and realising how much my Playschool Babies mean to me.

item.title: CORONATION OF KING CHARLES III
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I was part of an historic event which was seen by millions of people across the world. It was the Coronation of His Majesty King Charles 111 on the 6th May 2023 at Westminster Abbey. I along with several other people of colour were asked to take part in the ceremony. The diversity and inclusion […]
item.content:encoded: I was part of an historic event which was seen by millions of people across the world. It was the Coronation of His Majesty King Charles 111 on the 6th May 2023 at Westminster Abbey. I along with several other people of colour were asked to take part in the ceremony. The diversity and inclusion felt as though it was a glimpse into the future.

item.title: Order of Merit
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Two days before the late Queen Elizabeth II died she appointed me to the Order of Merit which is the highest honour in the land and is bestowed by the Monarch to only 24 living people.
item.content:encoded: Two days before the late Queen Elizabeth II died she appointed me to the Order of Merit which is the highest honour in the land and is bestowed by the Monarch to only 24 living people.

item.title: National Windrush Monument
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On 22nd June 2022 – ‘Windrush Day’ – the National Windrush Monument, which was created by Jamaican born artist Basil Watson, was unveiled at London Waterloo Station by two Windrush pioneers, Alford Gardner and John Richards, in the presence of The Prince and Princess of Wales. The Monument stands close to platform 19 where I […]
item.content:encoded: On 22nd June 2022 – ‘Windrush Day’ – the National Windrush Monument, which was created by Jamaican born artist Basil Watson, was unveiled at London Waterloo Station by two Windrush pioneers, Alford Gardner and John Richards, in the presence of The Prince and Princess of Wales. The Monument stands close to platform 19 where I arrived from Trinidad sixty-two years earlier, aged ten. Thousands of other Caribbean people also arrived at Waterloo Station between 1948 and 1971 before dispersing to cities across Britain.

item.title: My latest book
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: My latest ‘Coming to England’ book, a version for 4 year olds, comes out on the 8th October and I’m very excited. I always say ‘childhood lasts a lifetime’, so the earlier young children are conscious of the emotions they go through when you are different, no matter what that might be, the better it […]
item.content:encoded: My latest ‘Coming to England’ book, a version for 4 year olds, comes out on the 8th October and I’m very excited. I always say ‘childhood lasts a lifetime’, so the earlier young children are conscious of the emotions they go through when you are different, no matter what that might be, the better it is to be all embracing and have empathy for differences. Tolerance, compassion and consideration are the key to happiness.

item.title: My Statue at Exeter University
item.dc:creator: Keith
item.description: Here I am with my statue which was commissioned by Exeter University when I stepped down as Chancellor after 10 years. I hear some students give it a hug if they need a bit of comfort a solace. I love it!
item.content:encoded: Here I am with my statue which was commissioned by Exeter University when I stepped down as Chancellor after 10 years. I hear some students give it a hug if they need a bit of comfort a solace. I love it!

item.title: Damehood Medal
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On the 12th March 2020 I was thrilled to go to Buckingham Palace to collect my Damehood Medal, which I received for over 40 years of charitable work. Prince Charles presented it to me but we didn’t shake hands because of Covid-19, instead, we did a namaste greeting. A few days later the country went into lockdown.
item.content:encoded: On the 12th March 2020 I was thrilled to go to Buckingham Palace to collect my Damehood Medal, which I received for over 40 years of charitable work. Prince Charles presented it to me but we didn’t shake hands because of Covid-19, instead, we did a namaste greeting. A few days later the country went into lockdown.

item.title: Private Members Bill
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Read the Private Members Bill Here
item.content:encoded: Read the Private Members Bill Here

item.title: All Party Parliamentary Group
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I chair an All Party Parliamentary Group (APPG) on a Fit & Healthy Childhood which recently published a report entitled Healthy Patterns for Healthy Families: Removing the Hurdles to a Healthy Family. I believe the epidemic of childhood obesity should be treated as a national emergency. We need a minister at Cabinet level to coordinate […]
item.content:encoded:
I chair an All Party Parliamentary Group (APPG) on a Fit & Healthy Childhood which recently published a report entitled Healthy Patterns for Healthy Families: Removing the Hurdles to a Healthy Family. I believe the epidemic of childhood obesity should be treated as a national emergency. We need a minister at Cabinet level to coordinate strategy at all government levels. If you agree, write a letter to your MP to get change and make a difference.

Click HERE to read the report.

The report on Maternal Obesity, published in June 2017, is available here.

The report on Physical Education is available here.

Our critique of the National Obesity Framework is available here.

Our report on Play is available here.

Food in Schools and the Teaching of Food, our third report, is available here.

Our report, The Early Years, is available here.

The report on Healthy patterns for healthy families: clearing the hurdles to a healthy family is available here.

item.title: Baroness Doreen Lawrence
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I was a supporter for Baroness Doreen Lawrence when she was introduced to the House of Lords on the 15th October 2013. What an inspirational day that was.
item.content:encoded: I was a supporter for Baroness Doreen Lawrence when she was introduced to the House of Lords on the 15th October 2013. What an inspirational day that was.

item.title: Hay Festival
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: This year I was part of the Hay Festival. One of the biggest literary festivals in country.The Glastonbury of the Mind. I was talking about my book, Coming to England, on the 20th anniversary of it’s publish by Pan Macmillan, and loved every moment of being part of this wonderful festival. Managed to get some […]
item.content:encoded:
This year I was part of the Hay Festival. One of the biggest literary festivals in country.The Glastonbury of the Mind. I was talking about my book, Coming to England, on the 20th anniversary of it’s publish by Pan Macmillan, and loved every moment of being part of this wonderful festival.

Managed to get some time to speak to Kalpana Boodhoo‏, for a lovely interview – listen below…

Listen here: https://audioboom.com/posts/5989571-floella-part-1

and here: https://audioboom.com/posts/5989574-floella-part-2

item.title: Spring Watch: Unsprung
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Adored being on BBC Springwatch Unsprung, talking about children being encouraged to link up with nature. It was great spending time out in the country with Chris Packham, where I felt spiritually at home. Link to Springwatch Unsprung
item.content:encoded:
Adored being on BBC Springwatch Unsprung, talking about children being encouraged to link up with nature. It was great spending time out in the country with Chris Packham, where I felt spiritually at home.

Link to Springwatch Unsprung

item.title: Children’s BAFTA Awards
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Had the wonderful evening at the 20th Children’s BAFTA Awards. I started the BAFTA Children’s awards and it has blossomed into the spectacular event it is now. I presented the BAFTA for Best Presenter.
item.content:encoded:
Had the wonderful evening at the 20th Children’s BAFTA Awards.

I started the BAFTA Children’s awards and it has blossomed into the spectacular event it is now.

I presented the BAFTA for Best Presenter.

item.title: Women of the Year Lunch
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Attended the annual Women of the Year Lunch, founded by the inspirational and visionary Lady ‘Tony’ Lothian This year we celebrated it’s Diamond Jubilee and it’s amazing to think the Lunch started honouring women’s achievements even before women could be made Peers the House of Lords back in 1958. Before the 1st Lunch Tony was […]
item.content:encoded: Attended the annual Women of the Year Lunch, founded by the inspirational and visionary Lady ‘Tony’ Lothian This year we celebrated it’s Diamond Jubilee and it’s amazing to think the Lunch started honouring women’s achievements even before women could be made Peers the House of Lords back in 1958. Before the 1st Lunch Tony was told you won’t get more than 50 women achievers, 500 were honoured at the Savoy in 1955. This year amongst the guests were Jenny Mayers pictured here with me, UK’s 1st black woman member of The Magic Circle. She represented just one of the wide range of professions and careers which women take on successfully today. Tony Lothian would have been happy to know that her legacy lives on and that her efforts were all worthwhile!

item.title: CROSSRAIL
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Being 30m under the streets of London made me realise what an incredible feat of engineering it is and how privileged we are to be able to make a difference to society’s well being.
item.content:encoded: Being 30m under the streets of London made me realise what an incredible feat of engineering it is and how privileged we are to be able to make a difference to society’s well being.

item.title: The Selfish Giant
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Returned to Jersey after 34 years to narrate Oscar Wilde’s ‘The Selfish Giant’ with the Jersey Chamber Orchestra conducted by the talented Daniel Cohen. It was a wonderful experience and I would love to perform this at the next Proms it’s a great piece for both children and adults alike. Last time I was in […]
item.content:encoded: Returned to Jersey after 34 years to narrate Oscar Wilde’s ‘The Selfish Giant’ with the Jersey Chamber Orchestra conducted by the talented Daniel Cohen. It was a wonderful experience and I would love to perform this at the next Proms it’s a great piece for both children and adults alike. Last time I was in Jersey was to film the 1st episode of Bergerac!

item.title: Barnardo’s Event
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I am a vice president of the wonderful children’s charity Barnardo’s and on the 10th June I hosted a reception at the Palace of Westminster to celebrate their work and bring together supporters, donors and anyone who cares about the welfare of children and families. I introduced the Minister for Children & Families Edward Timpson […]
item.content:encoded: I am a vice president of the wonderful children’s charity Barnardo’s and on the 10th June I hosted a reception at the Palace of Westminster to celebrate their work and bring together supporters, donors and anyone who cares about the welfare of children and families. I introduced the Minister for Children & Families Edward Timpson MP. I was so thrilled that some of our high profile supporters there too. Andy Serkis and his wife Lorraine Ashbourne as well as the fabulous Rosamund Pike.

item.title: Tobago
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Tobago is a wonderful island in the Caribbean and I just returned from a lovely official visit there. I was greeted with parades and even had to inspect a guard of honour! But the most overwhelming welcome was when I arrived at the airport where they had arranged the most fantastic welcoming party with dancers […]
item.content:encoded:
Tobago is a wonderful island in the Caribbean and I just returned from a lovely official visit there. I was greeted with parades and even had to inspect a guard of honour! But the most overwhelming welcome was when I arrived at the airport where they had arranged the most fantastic welcoming party with dancers and a steel band. Everywhere I went I was greeted with great love and affection.
I spoke to schoolchildren, teachers and women’s groups all over the island.

It was a tough schedule but very rewarding. I can’t wait to go back.

Click here to see more photos.

item.title: Monaco Grand Prix
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: As soon as I got back I was off to the Monaco Grand Prix to see Lewis Hamilton race. One of the highlights of the weekend was meeting a woman I have always admired, the great Shirley Bassey…
item.content:encoded: As soon as I got back I was off to the Monaco Grand Prix to see Lewis Hamilton race. One of the highlights of the weekend was meeting a woman I have always admired, the great Shirley Bassey…

item.title: Royal Geographical Society
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Next stop was the Royal Geographical Society in Kensington where I was interviewed by the lovely Sian Williams … had the most inspirational evening meeting and chatting to the audience.
item.content:encoded: Next stop was the Royal Geographical Society in Kensington where I was interviewed by the lovely Sian Williams … had the most inspirational evening meeting and chatting to the audience.

item.title: Exeter Cathedral School
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On the 5th May I had the honour and privilege to open Exeter Cathedral School’s new nursery. I told the children the story of the Three Little Pigs and the I unveiled a plaque in front of all the parents, staff and governors. It was a lovely day!
item.content:encoded:
On the 5th May I had the honour and privilege to open Exeter Cathedral School’s new nursery.

I told the children the story of the Three Little Pigs and the I unveiled a plaque in front of all the parents, staff and governors. It was a lovely day!

item.title: The University Tremough campus
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: The next day I went on to Cornwall and visited The University Tremough campus there. As part of the visit I went underground at the Cambourne School of Mines. What a fabulous experience that was, the tunnels are carved out of solid granite and are stained with mineral deposits of iron and copper which are […]
item.content:encoded:
The next day I went on to Cornwall and visited The University Tremough campus there.

As part of the visit I went underground at the Cambourne School of Mines. What a fabulous experience that was, the tunnels are carved out of solid granite and are stained with mineral deposits of iron and copper which are beautiful.

The acoustics down there are fantastic and I sang a few bars of ‘Blues is my Business’ before I detonated an explosion! Yes my life is very exciting.

item.title: Draper’s Hall in the City of London
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On the 19th March I was guest speaker at Draper’s Hall in the City of London. The audience was made up of students, lecturers, educationalists and teachers from the many schools the Draper’s Company support. What a great organisation they are.
item.content:encoded: On the 19th March I was guest speaker at Draper’s Hall in the City of London. The audience was made up of students, lecturers, educationalists and teachers from the many schools the Draper’s Company support. What a great organisation they are.

item.title: Hull as the City of Culture
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On the 20th March I was in Hull to support the Lib-Dem candidate for Hull West & Hessle, Claire Thomas. I was on the panel which chose Hull as the City of Culture for 2017 so I made a speech about why we though Hull was the best choice. We gathered at the One Gallery, […]
item.content:encoded: On the 20th March I was in Hull to support the Lib-Dem candidate for Hull West & Hessle, Claire Thomas. I was on the panel which chose Hull as the City of Culture for 2017 so I made a speech about why we though Hull was the best choice. We gathered at the One Gallery, Humber Quays Some lovely works on display there.

item.title: Battersea Sports Stadium
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On Saturday 28th Feb I went along to Battersea Sports Stadium and gave an inspirational talk to all the wonderful runners who will be taking part in this year’s London Marathon and raising money for Barnardo’s. Later on I went to the Lib-Dem Conference in Reading and gave them an inspirational talk as well!
item.content:encoded:
On Saturday 28th Feb I went along to Battersea Sports Stadium and gave an inspirational talk to all the wonderful runners who will be taking part in this year’s London Marathon and raising money for Barnardo’s.

Later on I went to the Lib-Dem Conference in Reading and gave them an inspirational talk as well!

item.title: Meet Tate Britain
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On the 9th February I hosted a tour around Tate Britain, called ‘Meet Tate Britain’. I talked about the representation of childhood in Art and I was surprised to find so many paintings at the Tate with children in them. A lovely group of people turned up and we had a brilliant time walking round […]
item.content:encoded:
On the 9th February I hosted a tour around Tate Britain, called ‘Meet Tate Britain’. I talked about the representation of childhood in Art and I was surprised to find so many paintings at the Tate with children in them.

A lovely group of people turned up and we had a brilliant time walking round the gallery. I ended up hugging them all.

It was a most enjoyable, inspirational and thought provoking evening.

item.title: Victoria International School
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On the 17th February I visited one of my favourite schools, the Victoria International School of Sharjah in UAE. I visited the infant’s school where I sang some of my old Playschool action songs and interacted with the young children. I love their joy and excitement for the world. I then did an inspirational talk […]
item.content:encoded:
On the 17th February I visited one of my favourite schools, the Victoria International School of Sharjah in UAE. I visited the infant’s school where I sang some of my old Playschool action songs and interacted with the young children. I love their joy and excitement for the world.

I then did an inspirational talk to the students who will be soon leaving for university. They were the most incredibly diverse and multi-national group of very special young people who are going to go out to make a real difference and change the world for the better.

item.title: The Arms of Britannia
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I remember vividly 50 years ago my head teacher Mrs Bowles, a stout woman of vision, arranging for us to watch on television, the funeral of Sir Winston Churchill. I wrote about this emotional occasion in my book ‘The Arms of Britannia’ The book also covers many other historical incidents that happened during my teenage […]
item.content:encoded:
I remember vividly 50 years ago my head teacher Mrs Bowles, a stout woman of vision, arranging for us to watch on television, the funeral of Sir Winston Churchill. I wrote about this emotional occasion in my book ‘The Arms of Britannia’

The book also covers many other historical incidents that happened during my teenage years in the 60s. Like most teenagers, some were funny others traumatic but they all had a great influence on my life.

Now at the age of 65 I find myself looking back and reflecting on the past. That’s why I’m so pleased to have written this book as my gift to young people to help them understand modern history and incidents that shaped their lives today.

item.title: Len Garrison Memorial Lecture
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On the 20th November I had the honour and privilege of giving the inaugural ‘Len Garrison Memorial Lecture’ at the Black Cultural Archives in Brixton. There was a great audience on the night which included Len’s family. Len was a great man who I met over 30 years ago. The new building is a legacy […]
item.content:encoded:
On the 20th November I had the honour and privilege of giving the inaugural ‘Len Garrison Memorial Lecture’ at the Black Cultural Archives in Brixton. There was a great audience on the night which included Len’s family.

Len was a great man who I met over 30 years ago. The new building is a legacy to his vision.

item.title: Lord Michael Grade and Tim Weber
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On the 19th Feb 2014 I was presenting the annual awards for the British Kinematograph Sound & Television Society (BKSTS). Winners included Lord Michael Grade and Tim Weber for his work on BAFTA winning film Gravity. Suddenly they sprung a wonderful surprise on me by announcing I was to be made an Honorary Fellow of […]
item.content:encoded:
On the 19th Feb 2014 I was presenting the annual awards for the British Kinematograph Sound & Television Society (BKSTS). Winners included Lord Michael Grade and Tim Weber for his work on BAFTA winning film Gravity.

Suddenly they sprung a wonderful surprise on me by announcing I was to be made an Honorary Fellow of the Society! As you can see I was delighted to be recognised by the Society, which is made up of technicians and craftsman & women from the film & television industry. My dear friend Sir Sydney Samuelson presented me with the honour.

item.title: CSR Waitrose
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I have just been appointed to the CSR (Corporate Social Responsibility) Board of Waitrose where I hope to help them further with all the good work they already do.
item.content:encoded: I have just been appointed to the CSR (Corporate Social Responsibility) Board of Waitrose where I hope to help them further with all the good work they already do.

item.title: Shakespeare Birthplace Trust
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I have been appointed an Honorary Fellow of the Shakespeare Birthplace Trust in Stratford-upon Avon. I passionately believe children and young people should be introduced to the works of Shakespeare. Here is a link to their website. Click here to see their website.
item.content:encoded: I have been appointed an Honorary Fellow of the Shakespeare Birthplace Trust in Stratford-upon Avon. I passionately believe children and young people should be introduced to the works of Shakespeare. Here is a link to their website. Click here to see their website.

item.title: Royal Air Force Museum
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On the first of November 2013, I opened a fantastic exhibition at the Royal Air Force Museum in North London called ‘Pilots of the Caribbean’. The exhibition commemorates the brave men and women of the Caribbean who joined the air force and fought for Britain in the 1st and 2nd World Wars.
item.content:encoded: On the first of November 2013, I opened a fantastic exhibition at the Royal Air Force Museum in North London called ‘Pilots of the Caribbean’. The exhibition commemorates the brave men and women of the Caribbean who joined the air force and fought for Britain in the 1st and 2nd World Wars.

item.title: Graduation at Exeter Uni
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Graduation at Exeter Uni was another spiritual and inspirational year with graduates promising not only to ‘Change the world’ but ‘Save the world’. And I think they are determined to do just that. I just loved singing for them at their Graduation Ball
item.content:encoded: Graduation at Exeter Uni was another spiritual and inspirational year with graduates promising not only to ‘Change the world’ but ‘Save the world’. And I think they are determined to do just that. I just loved singing for them at their Graduation Ball

item.title: Positano, Italy
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: In July me and the band did a gig in Positano, Italy. It’s a really beautiful place. The stage was set up on the beach and hundreds of people turned up to watch us rock.
item.content:encoded: In July me and the band did a gig in Positano, Italy. It’s a really beautiful place. The stage was set up on the beach and hundreds of people turned up to watch us rock.

item.title: Professor Tim Niblock
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I went to Sharjah to award an Exeter honorary degree to Professor Tim Niblock who is highly respected there. I also visited the Victoria International School and met the children and teachers.
item.content:encoded: I went to Sharjah to award an Exeter honorary degree to Professor Tim Niblock who is highly respected there. I also visited the Victoria International School and met the children and teachers.

item.title: Black History Month
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I have been visiting schools and libraries across the country speaking about Black History Month. It’s been so inspiring and rewarding to see the reaction of so many children and adults when I speak about the adversities many people had to go through and are still going through. The beauty of Black History Month is […]
item.content:encoded:
I have been visiting schools and libraries across the country speaking about Black History Month. It’s been so inspiring and rewarding to see the reaction of so many children and adults when I speak about the adversities many people had to go through and are still going through. The beauty of Black History Month is that it focuses our attention on the cultural diversity of our nation and how we must all endeavour to live together in peace and harmony.

Nick Clegg wrote a comprehensive piece about Black History Month. Click to see it.

I also spoke at the Henley Literary Festival about my book ‘Coming to England’ to mark Black History Month.

item.title: Women of the Year Lunch
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Another wonderful Women of the Year Lunch was celebrated at the Intercontinental Hotel, Park Lane. A stunning array of inspirational women. Click here..
item.content:encoded: Another wonderful Women of the Year Lunch was celebrated at the Intercontinental Hotel, Park Lane. A stunning array of inspirational women. Click here..

item.title: Exeter University Visit
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: Her Majesty the Queen and the Duke of Edinburgh visited Exeter University where I am the Chancellor to celebrate her Diamond Jubilee and open our stunning new Forum
item.content:encoded: Her Majesty the Queen and the Duke of Edinburgh visited Exeter University where I am the Chancellor to celebrate her Diamond Jubilee and open our stunning new Forum

item.title: When I’m 65
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: I was 65 on 23rd September 2014 and I intend to grow old stylishly!
item.content:encoded: I was 65 on 23rd September 2014 and I intend to grow old stylishly!

item.title: Action for Children’s Arts
item.dc:creator: Rob
item.description: On the 20th of October 2012, I had the honour of being awarded the J.M Barrie Award by Action for Children’s Arts. The Action for Children’s Arts J.M.Barrie Award is given annually to a children’s arts practitioner or organisation whose work, in the view of ACA, will stand the test of time. It was a […]
item.content:encoded:
On the 20th of October 2012, I had the honour of being awarded the J.M Barrie Award by Action for Children’s Arts.

The Action for Children’s Arts J.M.Barrie Award is given annually to a children’s arts practitioner or organisation whose work, in the view of ACA, will stand the test of time. It was a fantastic day that I was so happy to share with my family and many wonderful friends.

item.title: I love to do motivational, inspirational speeches.
item.dc:creator: Rob
